# WoodsOS — Muskoka Woods operations platform

> WoodsOS runs every part of camp in one place: bookings & leads, billing,
> catering, accommodation & housekeeping, facilities tickets with two-way guest
> chat, tasks & seasonal checklists, gear checkout, budgets & expenses, safety
> incidents, lost & found, sign-out, shopping & town runs, people & growth
> (kudos, pulse, 1:1s, goals, feedback, certifications), smart screens, a 3D
> property map, and per-area metrics. Access is governed by a three-layer
> permission system (module switches → role matrix → per-person overrides).

WoodsOS is AI-native. The supported way for an AI agent to use it is the
built-in MCP server — no API keys or credits, just a staff member's personal
access token.

## Connect (MCP)

- Endpoint: POST /api/mcp (streamable HTTP, stateless JSON-RPC 2.0)
- Two auth methods, both resolving to a WoodsOS user (the token IS the person):
  1. **Personal access token** — `Authorization: Bearer wos_pat_…`, minted in the
     app under **Claude & AI** (`ai.use` permission). Best for Claude Code.
  2. **OAuth 2.1** — for Claude.ai chat and Cowork "custom connectors". The MCP
     endpoint advertises discovery via `WWW-Authenticate` on a 401; metadata at
     `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` and
     `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`. Public clients, PKCE S256, dynamic
     client registration at `/api/oauth/register`, authorize at `/api/oauth/authorize`,
     token at `/api/oauth/token`. The person signs in + approves; access scoped
     to their permissions.
- Claude Code: `claude mcp add --transport http woodsos <origin>/api/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer wos_pat_…"`
- Claude.ai chat / Desktop / Cowork: in Claude, Customize → Connectors → "+" →
  Add custom connector → paste `<origin>/api/mcp` → Add, then sign in + approve.
  Enable it per conversation via the "+" menu (lower left) → Connectors.
  Team/Enterprise: an owner adds the URL under Organization settings →
  Connectors (Add → Custom → Web); members click Connect. Requires a public
  HTTPS origin.
- Tools mirror the holder's live permissions: `tools/list` only returns what they
  may do, write tools additionally require `ai.write`, and every write is audited
  under their name.
- Start with the `whoami` tool, then `daily_brief` for today's picture.
  `export_dataset` pulls whole datasets (JSON/CSV); `get_metrics` summarizes
  any area the holder can see.

## Humans

- App: / (sign in) · installable PWA
- Guest report form: /report/<token> (QR posters in every building)
- Guest ticket tracking + chat: /track/<token>
- Lobby screens: /screen/<token> · Lodging board: /board/<token>
- Bookings iCal feed: /api/public/ical/<token>

## Notes for agents

- Rate limits apply (HTTP 429 + Retry-After). Be gentle.
- IDs: tickets use MW- codes, incidents INC-, bookings BK- — tools accept either
  the code or the numeric id.
- Dates are camp-local (America/Toronto), ISO formatted.
